behaviors.description: Request includes full taxonomy labels in outgoing API payload.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff (classifier -> API client payload), not pure local logic. An in-process fake client that captures the dispatched request can detect missing labels. Rejected System because behavior text only says "request sent to the external API...includes...labels" and does not require real network/OS/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this behavior validates request construction, not model decision quality or multi-step agent chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a stub transport/client, call classify(document, taxonomy), and assert captured outbound payload contains the complete taxonomy list every time.

behaviors.description: Out-of-taxonomy API label is mapped to "unknown".
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when API response parsing/normalization is wired to caller-facing return path. A stubbed API returning an invalid label is sufficient to catch forwarding bugs. Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly spans API-returned value to module return contract. Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on real model quality; injected deterministic invalid response is enough. Rejected System since no real network is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API response with label "nonsense_label" and assert classify(...) returns "unknown" to caller.

behaviors.description: Normalization is deterministic for identical raw API responses.
behaviors.justification: This is local deterministic transformation logic: same raw input -> same normalized output. No module wiring or I/O is required to expose failure. Rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically about "module's normalization step" and can be tested directly as a pure function/object method.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization with the same raw response fixture multiple times and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: Repeated live calls stay consistent unless provider response changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "repeated live API calls" and consistency tied to provider outputs, so real model behavior/non-determinism is in scope. A mock would hide real drift/variance. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run multiple real API classifications for the same document+taxonomy over a short window, record provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label changes only when raw provider output changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces configured token budget before dispatch.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is config/token-budget logic interacting with dispatch gating (truncate/reject before send). A fake client can verify no over-budget dispatch occurs, so real infra is unnecessary. Rejected System because behavior text says "before dispatch" and can be validated without real network. Rejected Agentic because this checks pre-call budgeting infrastructure, not model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, pass an oversized document, and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs and outbound request respects budget/dispatch rules.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage stays within configured per-call budget for typical document.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real provider-reported usage from real API execution. A mock can be forced to pass and would hide true provider tokenization/usage behavior. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires "provider-reported token usage" (real external model/API behavior). Rejected Workflow because only single-call classification behavior is validated, not multi-step agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API call(s) with representative document(s), read provider usage metadata, and assert reported tokens are <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: On rate-limit signal, module waits and retries instead of returning error.
behaviors.justification: Retry/backoff correctness is orchestration logic between API client, wait/backoff mechanism, and caller contract; deterministic stubs can reproduce rate-limit then success. Rejected System because real HTTP timing is not required to catch this stated failure mode. Rejected Agentic because behavior depends on transport error handling, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API to return rate-limit error first, success second; assert module delays/retries and eventually returns label rather than immediate error.
